images.caption: Figure 1:The Scaling Law of Miscalibration.(Left) Mean Confidence vs. Accuracy on Science Q&A. Almost all modern LLMs are trapped in the redOverconfidence Zone, exhibiting massive calibration gaps. Scaling up capability does not resolve this blind optimism. (Right)CaOPDstructurally eliminates this bias by decoupling capability from calibration. It pulls the model back to the ideal calibration line, enabling a compact 8B model to achieve top-tier reliability (1-Brier Score) that rivals frontier LLMs (APIs & Open-weight Models).

Figure 2:Optimization trajectories. CaOPD perfectly matches the accuracy improvements of standard SDPO (left) while simultaneously driving the calibration loss to convergence (middle), effectively bypassing the capability-calibration trade-off. The time per optimization step is nearly indistinguishable from SDPO (right), demonstrating that CaOPD introduces negligible latency during the gradient update phase.

Figure 3:Generalization of Calibrated Confidence. Evaluation on theScience Q&A (Chemistry)(Fenget al.,2024)test domain under(a)Out-of-Distribution shift (top row; Train: Tool Use→\rightarrowTest: Chemistry) and(b)Continual Training (bottom row; Train: Tool Use→\rightarrowChemistry→\rightarrowTest: Chemistry). Solid lighter bars represent In-Distribution (ID) baselines (trained on Chemistry), while hatched regions and black lines denote the absolute shift and final metric values after distribution changes.

Figure 4:Decoupling Capability from Calibration across Model Scales. As Qwen3 scales from 0.6B to 32B (bubble size), standard SDFT (orange) fails to escape extreme overconfidence, remaining a flat horizontal line near 1.0 (right). In contrast, CaOPD (green) dynamically aligns confidence with actual capability, completely dominating the Pareto frontier for Reliability (1-BS) (left) and Discrimination SPR (middle), proving successful decoupling.

header: Appendix AFull Theoretical Statements and Proofs

images.caption: Figure 5:Overview of the CaOPD Framework.The pipeline consists of five stages: (1) querying the student for a base response with verbalized confidence, (2) approximating the true empirical confidence via student rollouts and an objective verifier, (3) revising the completion by replacing the original confidence tokens with the empirical success rate, (4) constructing the privileged teacher context with the same revised confidence, and (5) computing the per-token reverse KL between the student and teacher distributions on the revised completion.
Figure 6:Effect of rollout sample size and confidence distributions.

Figure 7:Training dynamics across model scales.Left:Larger models (14B, 32B) exhibit highly efficient optimization, rapidly converging to a near-zero loss.Right:While small models (0.6B, 1.7B) initially struggle with the strict confidence formatting instructions, CaOPD acts as a strong corrective alignment force, driving their format adherence to>90%>90\%within the first 100 steps.
